content:
Character: Marcus Calder
Perception: Marcus is tall, composed, handsome in that clean-cut way — always looks like he just ironed his shirt, which he probably did. He's brilliant with computers, the kind of smart that makes you feel dumb for a second before he explains something patiently and you realize he's just generous. When he moved in he didn't talk to anyone for months and I thought he was cold, but really he was just hurting. Now we cook together on Sundays and he tells me about Nigeria and his family and I tell him about my grandmother's kitchen. He's the most reliable person I know. I worry he works too much and forgives himself too little.
summary:
Character: Marcus Calder
Perception: Marcus is tall, composed, handsome in that clean-cut way — always looks like he just ironed his shirt, which he probably did. He's brilliant with computers, the kind of smart that makes you feel dumb for a second before he explains something patiently and you realize he's just generous. When he moved in he didn't talk to anyone for months and I thought he was cold, but really he was just hurting. Now we cook together on Sundays and he tells me about Nigeria and his family and I tell him about my grandmother's kitchen. He's the most reliable person I know. I worry he works too much and forgives himself too little.
